class_schedule.topic: The Communication Process in a Digital World
learning_objectives: Define the elements of the communication process
Distinguish between synchronous and asynchronous digital communication
Identify the role of digital channels in modern communication
readings: Digital Communication: Principles and Practice Ch. 1
The Shift from Analog to Digital Communication (article)

lecture_notes.introduction: Communication is the foundation of human interaction. In this course we examine how digital technologies have transformed the way we create, share, and interpret messages.
key_concepts: Noise and feedback in digital contexts
Media richness theory applied to digital channels
The evolution from print to digital media
lecture_notes.demonstration: Comparison of the same message delivered via email, video call, and social media post
setup_requirements: Access to course learning management system
Web browser with video conferencing support

review.value: Sender encodes a message, transmits through a channel, receiver decodes; feedback completes the loop
Email, instant messaging, social media, video conferencing, collaborative documents

class_schedule.topic: Email Etiquette and Professional Writing Online
learning_objectives: Compose professional emails with clear subject lines and structure
Apply tone and register appropriate to academic and business contexts
Recognize common pitfalls in digital written communication
readings: Digital Communication: Principles and Practice Ch. 2
Business Email Best Practices (Harvard Business Review)

lecture_notes.introduction: Email remains the dominant medium for professional communication. Mastering its conventions signals credibility and competence.
lecture_notes.demonstration: Workshop rewriting poorly written emails to meet professional standards

review.value: Should be specific, concise, and action-oriented, e.g., 'Meeting Rescheduled to Friday 3 PM'
Without vocal cues, readers may interpret neutral text as curt; use polite openers and closings

class_schedule.topic: Social Media Communication and Personal Branding
learning_objectives: Analyze how social media platforms shape message construction
Develop a personal branding strategy across platforms
Evaluate the permanence and reach of online messages
readings: Digital Communication: Principles and Practice Ch. 4
Building Your Personal Brand Online (Forbes article)

lecture_notes.introduction: Social media has created new norms for public communication. Understanding platform conventions and audience expectations is essential for effective digital citizenship.
key_concepts: Personal branding across LinkedIn, Twitter, and portfolio sites
lecture_notes.demonstration: Audit of a sample LinkedIn profile with class discussion on improvements
setup_requirements: LinkedIn account (can be new or existing)
Access to social media analytics tools

review.value: LinkedIn favors professional tone; Twitter rewards brevity and wit; Instagram is visual-first
Every post, comment, and like contributes to a permanent public record searchable by employers

learning_objectives: Apply principles of visual hierarchy to digital content
Create effective infographics and visual summaries
Understand accessibility requirements for visual media
readings: Digital Communication: Principles and Practice Ch. 6
Universal Principles of Design, selected entries

lecture_notes.introduction: Visuals process faster than text in the human brain. Effective digital communicators leverage images, color, and layout to reinforce their messages.
key_concepts: Visual hierarchy and the F-pattern reading model
Alt text and WCAG accessibility standards
lecture_notes.demonstration: Live creation of an infographic using Canva summarizing class survey data

review.value: Size, color, contrast, and position guide the viewer's eye through content in order of importance
Alt text describes images for screen readers; color contrast ratios must meet WCAG AA (4.5:1)

class_schedule.topic: Collaborative Digital Communication and Remote Teamwork
learning_objectives: Select appropriate tools for collaborative work
Apply best practices for virtual meetings and group projects
Manage conflict and miscommunication in digital teams
readings: Digital Communication: Principles and Practice Ch. 8
Remote Work Communication Best Practices (MIT Sloan)

lecture_notes.introduction: Remote and hybrid work environments demand deliberate communication strategies. Miscommunication in digital teams is the norm unless actively prevented.
key_concepts: Choosing the right tool: chat, email, video, shared docs
Meeting agendas and action items for virtual meetings
Shared document etiquette and version control
lecture_notes.demonstration: Simulated remote team meeting with structured agenda and follow-up action items

review.value: Urgent and simple: chat. Complex and non-urgent: email. Nuanced or emotional: video call
Write messages that stand alone without requiring immediate follow-up questions

learning_objectives: Apply narrative structure to digital content
Use persuasion techniques ethically in online communication
Create a short digital story using multimedia tools
readings: Digital Communication: Principles and Practice Ch. 10
Aristotle's Rhetoric in the Age of Social Media (journal article)

lecture_notes.introduction: Stories are how humans make sense of information. Digital platforms offer powerful new tools for storytelling, but the fundamentals of narrative and persuasion remain unchanged.
key_concepts: Three-act narrative structure in short-form content
Ethos, pathos, and logos in digital persuasion
lecture_notes.demonstration: Analysis of three viral digital campaigns examining narrative and persuasion techniques
setup_requirements: Access to video editing tool (iMovie, Clipchamp, or similar)

review.value: Setup establishes context, confrontation introduces tension, resolution delivers the message
Persuasion must be transparent; manipulation through deception or emotional exploitation is unethical

questions.question: Explain the difference between synchronous and asynchronous digital communication and give two examples of each.
questions.answer: Synchronous communication happens in real time where all parties are present simultaneously, such as video calls and live chat. Asynchronous communication does not require simultaneous participation, such as email and discussion forums. The key distinction is whether immediate response is expected.

questions.question: A colleague sends you the following email: 'hey need the report asap thx'. Rewrite this email to meet professional standards and explain three specific improvements you made.
questions.answer: Revised: 'Subject: Q3 Sales Report Needed by Friday. Hi [Name], Could you please send me the Q3 sales report by end of day Friday? I need it for the client presentation on Monday. Thank you for your help, [Your Name]'. Improvements: (1) Added a specific, descriptive subject line. (2) Used a proper greeting and closing. (3) Specified the deadline and reason for urgency instead of vague 'asap'.

questions.question: What is visual hierarchy, and how does the F-pattern reading model affect the layout of digital content?
questions.answer: Visual hierarchy is the arrangement of design elements to guide the viewer's attention in order of importance using size, color, contrast, and position. The F-pattern reading model, identified by eye-tracking studies, shows that users scan web content in an F-shape: first reading across the top, then scanning down the left side with shorter horizontal movements. This means the most important content should be placed in the top-left area and along the left margin.

questions.question: Your remote team has a complex disagreement about project direction. Which communication channel should you choose and why? Explain why at least one alternative would be less effective.
questions.answer: A video call is the best choice because complex disagreements involve nuance and emotion that benefit from nonverbal cues like facial expressions and tone of voice. Video also allows real-time back-and-forth discussion to resolve misunderstandings quickly. Email would be less effective because written text strips away vocal tone and can easily escalate conflict through misinterpretation. Chat would also be problematic because rapid-fire short messages do not allow for the careful articulation that disagreements require.

rubric.key: Selects video call with valid reasoning
Explains why an alternative is less effective
References nonverbal cues or media richness

major_assignments.title: Digital Communication Portfolio
major_assignments.description: Create a professional digital communication portfolio that demonstrates mastery of the communication channels and techniques covered in weeks 1-5. The portfolio must include examples of professional email, a social media personal brand audit and improvement plan, a visual infographic, and a short digital story.
objectives: Demonstrate professional writing across digital channels
Apply visual communication principles to create effective content
Show awareness of audience, tone, and platform conventions
Reflect on personal communication strengths and areas for growth
deliverables: Social media brand audit with before/after comparison
Original infographic on a topic of choice
60-second digital story video or slide deck
Reflective essay (500-750 words) analyzing your communication growth

criteria: Clear purpose and call to action in each email
Thorough audit of current digital presence
Clear growth areas identified with action plan
All four portfolio components completed to professional standards
Evidence of growth and reflection throughout the course
Consistent professional quality across all communication channels
Creative and thoughtful approach to each component

participation_and_activities.description: Active participation in discussions, workshops, and in-class activities

quizzes.description: Regular assessment of communication concepts and application

writing_assignments.description: Weekly writing exercises across various digital channels

major_portfolio.description: Comprehensive digital communication portfolio demonstrating skill mastery

final_presentation.description: Oral presentation of portfolio with live digital communication demonstration

course_policies.attendance: Regular attendance is expected. More than 3 unexcused absences will result in a 5% grade reduction per additional absence.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late work is penalized 10% per day up to 3 days. After 3 days, assignments receive a zero unless prior arrangements are made.
course_policies.collaboration: Most assignments are individual unless explicitly designated as group work. Peer review workshops require constructive, professional feedback.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All writing must be original. Proper citation is required for all sources. Undisclosed use of AI writing tools constitutes academic dishonesty.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students need reliable internet access, a university email account, and access to basic productivity software (word processor, presentation tool, and web browser).
